item.title: My first QM: From SMILES string to dihedral scan
item.description: This post goes over my first attempt at starting with nothing but a SMILES string, converting that into a molecule file with optimised geometry, and performing some basic QM calculations in Psi4 to do a dihedral scan. There are also a few notes on quantum chemistry, but be warned: having never formally studied the stuff, I may have made some extremely embarassing errors (and am very open to being corrected).

item.title: How to Use Flat-Bottomed Potentials in GROMACS
item.description: Flat bottomed potentials (FBPs) are a unique (and powerful) type of restraint we can apply to specific particles in a molecular dynamics simulation. In short, we define a fixed geometric region within the simulation box to which certain atoms will either be attracted or repelled. They’re also a little tricky to understand from a technical perspective. In this post, I’ll briefly discuss the maths, how to implement an FBP in the topology and restraints files, and finish up with some practical notes for usage.

About Me I’m an Australian PhD Student in computational biophysics at the Molecular Microbology and Structural Biochemistry (MMSB) unit of the Centre national de la recherche scientifique (CNRS), **based in Lyon, France. Specifically, I work on atomistic and coarse-grained molecular dynamics simulations of lipid droplets (and more). The biogenesis of these tiny organelles, fundamental to energy metabolism within the cell, is extremely difficult to study expexperimentally, due to their highly fluid nature and the fact that they often only consists of a few hundred molecules when they are first created.
